title: conformance-verdicts.v1 — the standing conformance sweep's machine channel
description: The structured payload the read-only audit persona appends (as one HTML-comment-wrapped block, invisible in the rendered digest) alongside its plain-language digest when it runs the standing product-spec-conformance sweep (issue #449). A follow-up tool (conformance_sweep.py promote) parses it, opens or updates one deduped engine issue per divergence, and strips the block before the digest is sealed — so the JSON is never committed and never feeds back into a later run as stale judgment. This is DISTINCT from audit-finding.v1 (the audit's own finding shape, a retire/reconcile/escalate recommendation): a conformance divergence is still reported as a reconcile finding in the digest; this block only carries the per-criterion verdicts a mechanical dedup needs. The digest prose remains the record; this block is a best-effort accelerator, so a consumer treats an absent, malformed, or duplicated block as a clean no-op, never a failure.

kind.description: A discriminator identifying this payload — today the only value is 'product-conformance'. Kept explicit so a stray or off-kind block is a clean no-op rather than a mis-parse.

items.description: The per-criterion verdicts. An empty array is valid (the persona ran the sweep and found no divergence). Only 'diverges' verdicts are promoted to a tracked issue; 'meets'/'unsure' are carried for completeness and the digest disclosure.

product-conformance-item.description: One per-criterion conformance verdict: which settled criterion it is (by the matrix row identity (doc, digest)), whether the built code still meets it, and the persona's plain-language note. The verdict is AI judgment with no behavioural correlate at cron cadence — the promoted issue says exactly that.

doc.description: The repo-relative, forward-slashed `docs/spec/` document path the criterion came from (the doc half of the matrix row identity).

digest.description: The criterion-cell content digest (the digest half of the matrix row identity — what the promoted issue dedups on, so recurring drift updates one draft rather than re-nagging).

verdict.description: The persona's read of whether the built code still meets this settled criterion. Only 'diverges' is promoted.

note.description: The persona's plain-language explanation of the verdict — what it checked and why it read the built code as diverging (or not). Author-influenced text; the consumer neutralises it before it lands in a rendered issue body.

criterion.description: The criterion text as the persona read it from the spec, for the operator's context in the promoted issue. Optional; author-influenced, neutralised on render.
